content:

This failed immediately...

  • I then right-clicked the icon, Quit Thunderbird & re-started it. No desktop window opened.
  • The only thing that worked from the right-click pop-up was the Write New Message function which did open a new email message.
  • I then uninstalled Thunderbird selecting the full uninstall option to get rid of any account data, & re-installed it.
  • Still the same problem: no main desktop window, & only Write New Message works.

Any advice on what may be wrong & how to fix it would be much appreciated.

In 2024 stelde iemand jullie dezelfde vraag als die ik heb: Ik wil dat berichten niet als conversaties worden weergegeven maar in volgorde van binnenkomst in de inkomende postbus verschijnt. Kan niet vinden hoe dat moet.

Dan komt de moderator met een best bewerkelijke en vreemde oplossing: zet in thunderbird de menubalk aan, rechtermuisknop klikken in een leeg stuk van de balk en kies menubalk. Als u in het menu dat nu zichtbaar is voor beeld kiest ziet u meer opties.

title: Thunderbird in Windows 11
creator: mico705
content:
Hi all.

I have just installed Thunderbird on my laptop, due to ongoing issues with using Outlook.

I have an MS Access database that I built using macros (I know, but it works perfectly fine for what I need!) I send emails with a signature and PDF attachments, or sometimes through a gateway (email to SMS), to inform guest about booking details at my small hotel.

Previously with Outlook, when I hit send on the database, it would pop the Outlook email window for me to review the mail before confirming send. Now I fin in Thunderbird, it doesn't display over the other apps, so I'm having to minimise or close the DB window, to review and send the mail.

I have already set Thunderbird as the default app in the setting menu, for all mail tasks and file types, and can't seem to find any other setting to allow it to display on top.

I was therefore wondering if anyone else has encountered a similar issue, and if they have, whether they were able to solve it before pulling out too much of their hair!

Any help would be much appreciated, thank you.

Mick Courcier

title: Archiving Thunderbird emails to "Other" Local Folder custom archive folder is ignored - archive still uses default Thunderbird Local Folders "Archive" folder
creator: EMC_Hirf
content:
I've set up email archiving for three IMAP accounts. Initially each account used its default Local Folders archive folder path (i.e. Account Settings > Copies & Folders > Message Archives), I had selected the first option, 'Keep message archives in "Archives" folder on Local Folders'. For the archive options, I selected to retain the original email account folder structure and to organize archived messages by year. This all worked fine but it resulted in a combined group of archived messages without any obvious separation of emails by IMAP account. I prefer to have emails archived and grouped according to email account just like they are organized in the main Thunderbird email account and folder hierarchy.

I then noticed the Account Settings archive messages option to select "Other" where I could point the archive to a custom-named folder on Local Folders. Hence, I created three new Local Folders, named "Archive-Gmail", "Archive-Outlook.com" and "Archive-Polarismail", and then selected each of these "Other" archive local folders in their respective IMAP account's Archive settings. However, when I archived a bunch of emails from one of the folders in my Polarismail account, the emails got archived back into the default Thunderbird Local Folders > Archives folder (which I had deleted, but it got recreated). That is, this new archive was stored under the folder tree "Local Folders/Archives/<foldername>/<year>".

Thinking that perhaps Thunderbird didn't like my use of the "-" for the archive folder name, I then tried renaming my Polarismail archive folder to "ArchivePolarismail" and then tried again to archive some emails from the Polarismail account. Again, the archive got placed in "Local Folders/Archives/<foldername>/<year>".

It seems that the "Other" option for creating a custom Local Folders archive folder and pointing the email account archive to it in the Settings doesn't work, at least not as I expect, in which case what function does this option serve? It appears that Thunderbird insists upon using/creating its archive folder with the "Archives" name, regardless of what parameters are set under the "Other" archive option.

I then briefly looked in the Thunderbird Config.editor settings and using the filter keyword "archive", found the following group of "archive" settings. There appear to be a bunch of old or redundant Archive folder location records since there are several entries for "mail.identity.id1.archive_folder" all of which are set to mailbox://nobody@Local%20Folders/Archives.

I don't recall if/when I might have created and deleted archive locations within Account Settings, but the multiple entries (each with a different id#) containing the setting mailbox://nobody@Local%20Folders/Archives suggests that this is the case.

I'm wondering if the order in which the mail.identity.id1.archive_folder entries are listed determines whether Thunderbird uses that particular entry or not and if Thunderbird is using the first few entries of the mail.identity.id1.archive_folder parameter to decide where to archive emails?

In any case, since I don't know what all this Config information does or affects, I'm afraid to haphazardly go about deleting any of the seemingly redundant entries - I would have expected Thunderbird to manage that from its Account Settings menu.

title: Thunderbird
creator: wherepond
content:
Hello When I upgraded my Kubuntu 20 to 24 recently, I lost Internet access. So I installed Kubuntu 22 and upgraded to Kubuntu 24 which restored my Internet access. Consequently I am now trying to restore my emails in Thunderbird from my last backup. It took me while to realize that relevant Thunderbird profiles.ini wasn't the one in the Home > hidden .thunderbird Rather the profiles.ini is in Home > snap >thunderbird > common> .thunderbird That snap fooled me for a while ! So documentation doesn't mention snap, anyway I found it eventually. Also some documentation mentions profile.ini whereas it is actually profiles.ini ... plural. Sorry to be niggly. So now my profiles.ini looks like this: [Profile1]

Name=default IsRelative=1 Path=y72gpdv6.default Default=1

[InstallFDC34C9F024745EB] Default=3f6mpdvc.default-release Locked=1

[Profile0] Name=default-release IsRelative=1 Path=3f6mpdvc.default-release

[General] StartWithLastProfile=1 Version=2

I discovered that putting y72gpdv6.default from my backup in the code block at the top like that enables me to see most of my emails when I run Thunderbird. There are 4 blocks of code. Could you please tell me that the blocks do? There are 3 defaults, in fact 1 default and 2 default-releases. What is the purpose of the 2 default-releases? Should I change them, for example replace them with something from my backup? Naturally I want to be sure that I have recovered everything. My original profiles.ini only has 3 blocks of code. Of course, I considered just replacing the new profiles.ini with the original profiles.ini from my last backup, BUT the new profiles.ini has 4 blocks of code instead of 3, hence I'm trying to understand how profiles.ini works. I would appreciate any help. Thank you. Ron H

title: Not able to send email using Spectrum email account.
creator: hayter14
content:
Running windows 11 in S mode, Spectrum email. I had been using Outlook but on 11 February, it stopped working. Wouldn't send nor receive. Webmail works fine. Downloaded Thunderbird. Set up my account and started receiving emails. Went to send an email, and it failed to send. Checked all settings and they match Spectrum's server settings.

Trying to troubleshoot, but struggled with directions on the internet. Tried to load developer tools and nothing happened. Found I could open the error console with keypad. The error console says "Could not start Browser Toolbox, you need to enable it." Could not find out how to enable it.

When I attempt to send email, three messages show up in the error console. "NetworkTimeoutError: a Network error occurred SmtpClient.sys.mjs:476:17" "SecurityError info: SmtpClient.sys.mjs:492:21" Note, no error text was displayed. "Sending failed: ... server (SMTP) timed out.... MessageSend.sys.mjs:341:32"

When I first started running Thunderbird, I remember a certificate error with the Spectrum certificate. I enabled an exception and was able to receive email. Thinking this might be related to the security error on receiving, I wanted to remove the certificate exception so I could see the reason for the security error on receiving. Comments say it is stored in the profile. If I try to open the profile, Thunderbird says profile not found. I found the profiles, but the path to them is not the path listed under Troubleshooting Information\About Profiles.

My primary issue is not being able to send emails. I would also like to know how to remove the certificate exception.

content:
I've had Thunderbird email account since the spring of 2014. The first devastating problem with Thunderbirt happened when I updated a new version of it about 2,5 years ago. 80% of my email addresses disappeared, about 80 % of both sent and received emails disappeared.

About 2,5 weeks ago I noticed that I was not receiving any emails on my Thunderbird account. I could see them in the webmail but not my Thunderbird account. I could send emails normally, but they would not appear in my SENT file. I can see in my Thunderbird account in a number change (Received emails) if I have received some, but none actually appear, I must go to my webmail to see them.

But to make this stranger, SOME emails do randomly appear in my received account on Thunderbird, perhaps 1 in 30 received emails.

Also, once again, about 80-90% of all my sent and received emails have randomly disappeared from those files.

I've spoken about this with my webmail provider and they tried all they could, but to no avail. We closed my Thunderbird account and opened it again and transferred all the emails (received and sent) from the webmail to the Thunderbird, and it took about 3 hours of transfer time, and it seemed to happen as the numbers were counting at the bottom left. But when all was transferred and I opened the Sent, Received and Notes files, they had disappeared again, nothing of the re-transfer actually stuck.

I'm telling you this because twice now in 2,5 years I've found that your Thunderbird email service does not function, it has crashed, it's been an absolute catastrophe, I waste my time with it, and now I have to fish old received and sent emails from the webmail.

You should fix these problems.

I'm changing my email address in a few days to gmail, so "thanks" and good-bye.

Markku Piri

title: Thunderbird136 won't send emails
creator: petermoore1
content:
after installing Tbird136 I could receive but not send emails. Some error message would flash but disappear to quickly to be read.

UPDATE - it turned out to be address book corruption due to the database updating T136 does. I started T136 and exported all my address books then went to the tbird profile and deleted all abook.sqlite files and restarted Tbird and sent emails w/o issues. reimported my exported address books and problem solved.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/134.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

I need to add them to a new mailing list, then after I've verified that they're valid and that the people do in fact want to get these emails, merge them into at least two of the existing mailing lists. I have them in a CSV file. First problem was finding where "create a mailing list" had moved - no longer on File - New in Address Book (why, by the way? That's the logical spot for it.). Then found that there's no way to import a file of addresses into a mailing list - they can only be imported into an address book. Ok, I'll import into a new address book, then create a mailing list, then drag and drop the contacts from the new address book into the new mailing list (which is both what I'd have expected to work, and what a support article I found said). Not what I wanted, because the new mailing list will be under the new address book, which is not where it needs to be, but I'll deal with that later. Nope - doesn't work. I can select any of or all the contacts, I can drag them, but everywhere on the panel has the "not permitted" symbol *except* another address book, which does me no good at all. Ok, try creating a new mailing list where I actually want it to be, then cutting and pasting the names and addresses from another file. Nope, can't find any way to cut and paste 2 fields into a mailing list in edit mode and have the data go where it needs to be, even a single contact, much less a bunch of them. It seems bizarre that something as fundamental as mailing lists would be as difficult to use as it seems, so I'm hoping I've missed something obvious. Currently on 128.7.0, BTW - I usually let TB update whenever it asks. Windows 10 on that box, but also pretty up to date.

title: How to Merge Thunderbird Junk Training in Two iMap Computers? (Merging Training.dat)
creator: Firefox.com73
content:
I'd be happy with an answer, but I offer a possible solution. Can someone tell me whether it will work?

When more than one copy of an iMap account exists, there is a problem with Junk mail learning. Namely, the learning only occurs on the computer where the user declares mail to be Junk, not on other copies of the same iMap account. It has been suggested that Training.dat be copied from one account to another, but that wipes out any training that has occurred on the copies that receive the new Training.dat file. A merge is needed.

So... Does THIS work? Go to the Junk folder and select all mail in it. Mark all the mail as Junk (seemingly redundantly). Unless Thunderbird makes a special case of NOT learning from marking mail as Junk when it already is, this ought to work, right?

title: (Thunderbird) View message in new window instead of new tab?
creator: nic18
content:
Hi,

Knowing this is the "ask the community" 's Thread, but don't know where else has official place to share about Thunderbird's info.

For those who wonder how to change this settings (View message in new window), I had searched up and down the net there is no new updated method has been shared.

It is easy and here is settings to change: Click at upper right corner resembles "Hamburger" icon > Settings > General > Scroll down till "Open messages in:" > Select "A new message window".

Done. You're welcome :)

title: mail inviate da thunderbird finiscono nello spam del destinatario

content:
Buongiorno, thunderbird ha sempre funzionato correttamente, in questi giorni qualsiasi mail che inoltro, rispondo o creo, la posta finisce nello spam del destinatario (anche se ho aggiornato il programma alla versione 136).

Non capendo se era un problema del dominio, magari inserito nella blacklist, ho verificato online su multirbl e mi da tutto verde, anche perché non invio nessuna massmail.

Poi la prova successiva è stata mandare la mail da "webmail" e li vanno a finire nella posta in arrivo del destinatario.

Secondo step, ho riconfigurato l'account su un altro client di posta e qui funziona (come webmail).

Come ho fatto le prove? Tramite le mie mail (diverse per dominio) e le mail dei colleghi (anche qui diverse per dominio).

Una volta visto questo che le mail arrivano da altri programmi, non so cosa posso fare su thunderbird, ho sempre usato thunderbird e ho tutta la posta li e non vorrei assolutamente cambiare client.

Magari se è capitato a qualcuno e può dirmi come ha risolto oppure qualche suggerimento anche modificando qualche variabile su thunderbird (Editor di configurazione...).

Grazie per ogni aiuto che possiate darmi!

Andrea

Dear Thunderbird Support Team,

title: Safe Attachments con Dynamic Delivery
creator: alessandro.masci1
content: Uso il client di posta Thunderbird per accedere tramite il protocollo IMAP a un account Office 365. Sul tenant è abilitato il "Safe Attachments" con Dynamic Delivery, pertanto il server exchange rimuove provvisoriamente l'allegato per l'analisi e lo rende disponibile dopo una ventina di secondi. Non ho problemi a ricevere messaggi con allegati provenienti da mittenti esterni. Se invece invio un messaggio contenente un allegato ad un utente esterno e a me stesso, il client Thunderbird scarica il messaggio nella posta in arrivo e al posto dell'allegato compare l'informazione "analisi degli allegati sicuri in corso", ma l'allegato non viene mai scaricato, anche a distanza di ore, anche chiudendo e riaprendo il client. Ovviamente il problema non si verifica se uso un client Outlook. title: Adaptive junk filtering thinks everything is junk until I tell it otherwise
creator: jsachs.sac
content:
I've just resumed using Thunderbird, with a new installation on a new computer, after a long period of having only my ISP's web interface. I'm having a problem with adaptive junk filtering: it thinks everything is junk until I tell it otherwise.

By default, it leaves putative junk messages in the Inbox, but displays a yellow ribbon above a junk message's preview with "Learn more" and "Not junk" buttons. In this configuration, having it assume everything new is junk is just a nuisance.

But eventually I will finish training it, and I'll want it to move junk to the Junk folder automatically. Then this behavior will be a disaster.

Using made-up numbers to illustrate, suppose I get 1000 messages per day. Of these, 998 are from addresses it recognizes, so it knows that 993 of them are junk and 5 are legitimate, because I've received email from those addresses before, and I've told it what to do with them. That leaves 2 messages which may or may not be junk.

It should leave those messages in the Inbox, preferably with some kind of warning, and let me decide whether they are junk or not. Instead it's going to move them to Junk, and I'll never know I got them unless I review all 995 new messages in the Junk folder... which would defeat the whole point of having a junk filter.

Is there a way to make the junk filter default to "not junk" when it doesn't know? I've looked at the configuration dialog, and I don't see one.

[Thunderbird 128] Is it possible to unify the INBOX and SENT folders belonging to one account?

I get a lot of e-mail requests to wildly varying topics and need to keep them organised by topic, date, etc.

Thus I often want to store incoming requests (single e-mails or threads) and my answer(s) to them together in an offline (local) folder common only to that case. I do this by traversing the INBOX and the SENT folder separately and then moving corresponding items manually to the same offline folder. Since I have a super-hierarchical and massive offline folder structure, it takes a lot of time to navigate to the same folder twice, but I do not see how I can avoid that. Unifying INBOX and SENT folders would be a way out, I then have to traverse the combined folder only once.

Maybe I am doing something wrong or have overlooked something trivial - I would be grateful if somebody could point that out, too.

title: UPDATE 136
creator: info6924
content:
Hello

Since the update to 136.0 Thunderbird I can no longer send emails and I can not go back to 128 because it refuses to refuse the data.

So I can call up emails with 136 but do not send emails. I spoke to the provider All settings are correct.

What can I do? With the 128 everything goes out of the problem

I am for quick help because I have a lot of contact with the hill by email and I do not ask for inquiries can answer.

Claudio Ulrich

title: "Sign in to Google" popup - argh!
creator: RealFamilyMan
content:
Please put Thunderbird back the way it was. I have several different email accounts in Thunderbird, I'm obviously logged into Google on one of those accounts, but for a while now TB constantly asks me to "Login to Google" - when I already am. i.e. It's asking me again and again, to login to one of the OTHER email/google accounts - and I don't want to, because I bet the moment I do, TB will popup the window again, wanting me to login to previous one. And so round and round we go.

Also the popup is poorly designed, because not all of it fits on the screen. i.e. It's oversized, so you can't see the top or bottom "bar" and there's no way I can see to adjust it. I detest this pointless popup, constantly appearing, having to first click on the white space, and then hit the ESC key to get rid of it (until next time). Why would you do this? We've always been able to have multiple email/gmail accounts, all with different login details, and all the email accounts still seem to be receiving email okay... so why add this annoying popup? Please remove it.

title: Calendars disappear when calendar cache is enabled
creator: vdkhnzpp8q
content:
Dear support team, I've the following issue with calendars in Thunderbird. Was on latest ESR version, an upgrade to latest version 136.0.1 (64 bit) didn't fix it: When I started Thunderbird, suddenly most of my calendars where no longer visible. I figured out by trying to add a new calendar it only (re-)appeared when "offline mode" (caching) was disabled. After changing the setting calendar.registry.[id].cache.enabled to "false" the calendars showed up again.

Could it be some kind of bug or could the calendar storage be corrupt? How can I fix it?

Many thanks in advance for an answer!

Kind regards, H. Bruecher

title: Templates don't open.  Cannot create a new message from a template.
creator: victor46
content:
I've been using Thunderbird for quite a while without any major issues, but yesterday, I ran into a problem that I cannot figure out.

I have a bunch of templates that I use regularly to create emails. The usual procedure is to click on a template email in the "Templates" folder, make the edits I need to the body content, modify the recipient information if necessary, hit send, and away goes a new message.

Yesterday, I click on a message in the Template folder, and nothing happens. The template message does not open up. If I right click and say "Create a new message from template" or whatever the exact prompt is, nothing happens. No new message is created.

I have tried creating a new message and saving it as a template, click on the new "template", and still nothing happens.

I have tried repairing the folder, using properties, but this is only offered if I click on the "Local" subfolder version, not on the "Unified" parent folder. No change.

I tried backing up the profile for security, deleting the "Template" message and index files, to try and see if recreating a new folder will work ... again, same results. Click on a template message and nothing happens. Right-click and select "create new message..." and nothing happens.

I have NOT tried creating a whole new profile yet since there are multiple email accounts, various custom search aggregations, and other customizations that have all been working just splendidly that I don't want to have to recreate all over if I can avoid it.

Running TBird 136.0 (64-bit) on Windows 11 which TBird reports is the latest / up-to-date version.

So far as I can tell, ALL of the other folders, sub-folders, local and IMAP, work as expected. I don't have this "message won't open" problem with any of them. This is affecting only templates and it's left me scratching my head.

Thank you in advance for your help, suggestions, and hopefully, solutions!

VictorZ

creator: btovrea
content: I would like to set a "default" background_image for the message preview pane when no message is displayed. Currently, the background_color I selected is displayed only if a message is selected (Oddly, this color is also displayed on other panes like Settings). A white background is displayed when no message is selected. Google search results usually include some config variable that does not appear in the list under config editor (deleted or deprecated?). Any ideas would be appreciated.

title: Moving Thunderbird to a new computer
creator: chebrou
content:
Hello, I am trying to move Thunderbird from my old Win 10 computer to a new Win 11 computer.

The problem: the accounts are moved but most are empty (no folder like Inbox, Drafts...) . See images.

I use the this how to: https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/moving-thunderbird-data-to-a-new-computer

I set the same version of Thunderbird on both PC: 128.8.0esr (32-bit) I also tried release versions 136 (64 bits) and 128 (64 bits) on the new computer: same result.

Some idea to solve this?

Best regards

title: Is/Could it possible to pin 'Favourites' in Thunderbird?
creator: bigthicko
content: I can't find a way to pin my 'Favourites' panel in Thunderbird. If it's not possible, then this can be regarded as a feature request. I have a few (4) definite 'Favourites' in my list, which I regularly have to move messages to. Since I have several email accounts, some of which have largish folder trees, I often scroll down to a different account, find I need to move a message to a 'Favourite' and then have to scroll all the way back up to locate the folder. In fact, I often go to the folder directly, rather than go all the way back to 'Favourites'. Making the idea of 'Favourites' a little redundant. Outlook has a simple 'Pin Favourites' feature that keeps the pane visible, even if the other folders are scrolled down. I find this incredibly useful, and would really like this ability in Thunderbird.

title: Disappearing Sent Folder
creator: TessWorld
content:
My Sent folder disappeared. How do I restore the folder with the sent messages still intact. Or, at the least, just restore an empty Sent folder. The In-Box, Drafts, and Trash are still there.

All the settings are correct, and it seems that because there's no Sent folder I cannot send an email. When I try to send a message I get the attached Error.

My local phone company routes email through Yahoo Mail, then to Thunderbird. It was all working fine until my Sent folder disappeared. Now because of the missing Sent folder I have to create the email message in Thunderbird saving it in Drafts so I have a record. Then, I have to copy it and send it from Yahoo Mail. Needless to say this is a pain in the you know what.

Can someone please provide the instructions to get my Sent folder back?

Thanks,

Tess

title: Thunderbird 136 Addon Compatibility
creator: electricdesert
content:
Hi team,

I tried switching over to the monthly release branch however none of my addons work.

Is there some major incompatibility introduced (aka WebExtensions)? I can not find any documentation anywhere as to what changed or why an extension would not work with 129+.

Also the release notes do not mention that 136 changes the Profile format, requiring a redownload of all messages from the server and therefore reindexing. This took several hours, only to find the addons don't work. Downgrading Thunderbird gives a profile error, and requires the `--allow-downgrade`switch to restore functionality. There should be a warning in the release notes regarding the one-way nature of the upgrade.

Thanks!

title: Thunderbird
creator: koenig.alexandra
content:
Dear Madam/Sir,

I have a problem with Thunderbird.

I receipt Emails and can login for view the emails, but I cannot send emails. There comes an field with information that the email could not send and to give in the password/a new password. "Anmeldung auf dem Server mail.gmx.net mit Benutzernamen "koenig.alexandra@gmx.net" fehlgeschlagen"

I do not understand what is the matter and I need your help. Because I do not need why I must give in a password for the Server. Also I do not know if I have a password therefore.

Is it possible that it is so because I had no connection from November until February and have reboot my rooter.

Please help me.

Best regards, Alexandra König

title: Signed but not encrypted messages are marked "invalid digital signature"
creator: thunderbird42
content:
When receiving signed but not encrypted messages, thunderbird complains with "invalid digital signature" and "technical error deteced". Unpacking the message and checking with gpg manualy verifies the signature correctly. Messages encrypted and signed are verified correctly. When looking at the logs in the console, I can see:

<< ----

openpgp: Parsed contentTypeLine: multipart/signed; micalg=pgp-sha256; protocol="application/pgp-signature"; boundary="------------95ynGFLP1eI6rMalg5HH0Iop" mimeVerify.sys.mjs:133:14 openpgp: Decrypting message: -----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE----- ..[snipped]

END PGP SIGNATURE-----

decryption.sys.mjs:143:14 openpgp: Nothing to decrypt/verify. decryption.sys.mjs:176:16 openpgp: Decrypting message: -----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE----- ..[snipped]

END PGP SIGNATURE-----

decryption.sys.mjs:143:14 openpgp: Nothing to decrypt/verify. decryption.sys.mjs:176:16 openpgp: Parsed contentTypeLine: multipart/signed; micalg=pgp-sha256; protocol="application/pgp-signature"; boundary="------------95ynGFLP1eI6rMalg5HH0Iop" mimeVerify.sys.mjs:133:14 openpgp: Found signed MIME message; micalg=pgp-sha256 mimeVerify.sys.mjs:148:16 openpgp: Parsed contentTypeLine: multipart/signed; micalg=pgp-sha256; protocol="application/pgp-signature"; boundary="------------95ynGFLP1eI6rMalg5HH0Iop" mimeVerify.sys.mjs:133:14 openpgp: Found signed MIME message; micalg=pgp-sha256

title: Current Thunderbird Version is a Disaster for Outlook import
creator: safox.2021
content:
This is not a question. It is a statement of issues. I recently bought a new computer and tried to install Thunderbird on my new system. The installation went fine. The setup was one problem after another. Thunderbird required multiple attempts to connect with Outlook and sync my account. Next, I tried to import my contacts. Thunderbird failed numerous attempts to sync contacts. I finally had to manually import my contacts. Then spent an hour manually cleaning up my contacts. Thunderbird seems to have randomly assigned field names. (Names imported as numbers or dates) In addition, it imported contacts without associated data. (No email address, phone numbers etc.) After all that, Thunderbird would not allow me to use any of the contacts. The address book is there, the contacts are there, but it would not reference the address book when I tried to send an email. I had to go to the address book, copy the email and paste it into the to: field.

Next, I tried to create events and tasks. First problem, all calendars were disabled by default and no option was offered to enable the calendars. I spent another 45 min. trying to find a setting or something to enable the calendars. When I finally got them enabled, I spent another hour trying to get Thunderbird to create events and tasks. Thunderbird will not create either. I spent far more time than is rational to create each event and task. When I completed each attempt, Thunderbird trashed them. No event or task was ever created. At this point I uninstalled Thunderbird and wrote it off as a bad idea.

Folks, I am not new to this. I have been using similar apps since their early days. I know how to install and setup these apps. I am very familiar with the problems that may be encountered. And this far worse than anything I have seen in a long time. I do not know what is going on at Mozilla, but they have got some serious problems to work out. I will steer clear of Thunderbird for now.

title: Hi guys. I have question.
creator: lee118
content:
For the longest time I used Eudora until the developers dropped it.

I did play with GMAIL for a while but I'm not too fond of having oligarchs know my every thought.

I even gave Outlook a trial for a couple of years but I grew tired of the damned add-on developers gouging me.

I tried Thunderbird and liked it a great deal. So much so that I happily make a monthly payment to show appreciation.

Okay, so, I switched to Windows Pro 11 and installed Thunderbird. I don't like it at all. Few controls match what I knew and I have better things to do with my days than go digging around to try to figure out what was perfectly comprehensible six months ago.

So, my questions are -

Is there any chance that you will ever add a nice simple button that says "Revert to Earlier Interface"

If that doesn't have a chance in hell of ever coming into being, can you suggest a decent email program that isn't from Big Coder and that can replace Thunderbird?

I'm not stupid or lazy - I worked in IT for 35 years - but I'm long past the point where trying to figure out what the other guy did to a favorite application was enjoyable. Stable and functional is good. New and mystifying is not.

Suggestions appreciated.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:136.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/136.0

Even if I double-click the email in the list to open it in a new tab, the contents are still not visible.

Emails sorted into other folders display without any issues. I tried right-clicking the affected folder → Properties → Repair Folder, but the issue remains.

Is there a way to fix this?

title: Newly installed Thunderbird on new PC's not receiving or sending email
creator: info12757
content:
I have installed Thunderbird on a new Lenovo PC using the latest version of Windows. I have copied the settings manually and correctly from a laptop in to the settings for the accounts on the PC. Thunderbird does not give any errors for collection of email though none are appearing in the inbox. There are messages on the server waiting to be collected.

There is a timeout on the SMTP when trying to send messages so no messages are sent.

I have checked all the settings and they are as follows: POP account port 110 smtp port 587 Using START and normal password authentication for both.

I have tried numerous other permutations of configurations as well as restarting Thunderbird after every change. As I mention these are the identical settings used on the laptops which the PC's are replacing. The PC is connected to the internet via ethernet cable and the internet functions fine.

Any ideas out there or anyone having had a similar experience and found a resolution?

title: Disappearing emails
creator: Janet1593
content:
I posted this before but have had no response whatsoever after several days. Maybe I posted it under the wrong thread, so am redoing it here. Apologies for repetition.

In the past week or so I have noticed that some emails disappear from one inbox on my Windows 10 PC. I have three Thunderbird accounts (call them A, B, and C), and the emails only disappear from box A. It seems completely random, and furthermore they only disappear on my own PC. My husband has an identical set-up on his Windows 10 PC, which also includes the boxes A, B, and C, and the messages do NOT disappear there. I can also see them on my iPhone and two iPads, and they do not disappear from those. I have tried forwarding them to myself from my phone, and while most of them did show up in the A inbox on my PC, I have noticed that some of them have now disappeared again.

This is incredibly frustrating, because I do most of my work at my PC. I thought I needed an update, but TB says I am up to date (128.8.0esr (32-bit)). It can't be anything to do with the server, because it is so specific: one inbox on one PC only. There doesn't seem to be any special pattern--some have attachments, some don't.

Many thanks for any suggestions!

I also posted two updates under the original question (which had a title something like 'Random emails keep disappearing'), and the problem is still ongoing, making my life a bit difficult, as I am at the moment in contact with doctors, lawyers, and insurers, and I keep losing their emails on my main PC, which holds documents that I sometimes need to attach to replies.

I am very surprised no one has replied (apart from my own updates), as usually there is someone out there with good ideas.

I'd really be grateful if someone could suggest something. Many thanks.

title: GoDaddy outlook.office365 Won't Get Messages
creator: 123buddyc
content:
Situation: Thunderbird 128.8.0esr (64-bit) has been performing flawlessly. No problems until I received a security notification that my email account had been accessed by someone quite literately on the other side of the world. And, since I haven’t changed my password in quite a while, I thought, ‘why not go ahead and do it now.’ It seemed like a good idea; not so much.

Prior to changing my password on my GoDaddy outlook.office.365.com email account, I have had absolutely no difficulties in sending and receiving emails from the ‘GoDaddy’ email account nor my gmail email accounts.

After changing only my ‘GoDaddy’ email account password, on the GoDaddy/outlook.offic365 website, I started having negative issues with the ‘GoDaddy outlook.office365’ email account on Thunderbird.


Conditions: At this time I was, and still am, able to send and receive emails on gmail accounts. No difficulties here.

However, I am only able to send emails on the ‘GoDaddy outlook.office365’ email account. I can not ‘Get Messages’ or receive new messages.

I also receive the following ‘notification’: User authenticated but not connected.


I have spent over 18 hours on the phone both voice and text attempting to resolve this issue to no avail.

I was told to change my settings to:

- input: outlook.office365.com. port 993, SSL/TLS, Oauth2 - Output: smtp.office365.com, port 587, STARTTLS, Oauth2

...which I had already set prior to contacting ‘GoDaddy Email Support Team’. After all this I was finally told to “contact Thunderbird to resolve this issue.”

I want to point out, that when I login to the GoDaddy email website via Firefox, I have not issues receiving / sending emails. I also have no issues using the ‘Outlook’ app on my android phone.

In an attempt to possible get a better ‘view point’ on the issue. I closed Thunderbird. Opened up a fresh download of Thunderbird AppImage and opened it in a ‘sandbox’ and ‘setup’ the GoDaddy email account. I still could only send and email. I was unable to ‘Get Messages’.

I have reviewed everything I could find on this forum that even came close to this issue, and, basically nothing changed.

If there is anyone who can provide some guidance, it would be greatly appreciated.

title: Problema con apertura posta PEC
creator: Neijek
content:
Thunderbird non apre email vecchia PEC. La email viene visualizzata come "vuota". Ma se viene salvata come .eml e si usa un qualunque sito o programma di lettura email .eml il contenuto e visualizzabile tranquillamente.

Debug provato:

  • Thunderbird v136.0
  • Thunderbird v128.8.0esr
  • Thunderbird v115.10.1
  • Modifica editor config mailnews.p7m_external true or false

Allego foto del email in Thunderbird e poi visualizzata in webmail o siti di lettura file .eml dove la email funziona perfettamente.

Altre tipo di pec funzionano, sembra che queste tipo di pec piu vecchia che includono un messaggio multiparte con vari allegati interni Thunderbird non riesce a visualizzarli.

title: Managing profiles with a third-party program
creator: sunhunter512
content: Hello! I am developing an application for an organization that uses Thunderbird in a Windows environment. Among other features, it is necessary to implement the transfer of mail client settings from one workstation to another. Built-in Mozilla Thunderbird tools are not suitable. I am still considering implementation options (autoconfig or direct work with files), and I have several questions. I will be very grateful for any help. 1. What is the minimum required files in the appdata folder that I need to create (transfer) so that the profile works without errors on the new workstation? No need to transfer messages, they are all on the server, and offline work is not used 2. How does the installs.ini file work? I need to avoid creating a new profile when starting Thunderbird for the first time, do I need to create an entry in installs.ini, and if so, where can I get the correct installation code? 3. Is it possible to transfer saved passwords to a new workstation? What are the requirements for this? I am very grateful for any answers

title: I'm suddenly unable to log into the ISP's Outgoing mail server
creator: tbird4
content:
I'm using 128.8.0esr (32-bit) Thunderbird on Windows 10. I've been using it for a very long time. Last night I was able to send and receive mail via Plusnet.

This morning I was getting the following message when I try to send:

Sending of the message failed. An error occurred while sending mail. The mail server responded: Authentication required. Please verify that your email address is correct in your account settings and try again."

I am the only user in this house. Nothing was changed during the night. I contacted the ISP, they checked all my account settings, none of which have been changed for a long time. All settings were as they expected. The ISP asked me to log into their web mail program and try sending mail. I was able to do this. At this point they ASSURED me that it is therefore a TBird problem.

I am a very experienced PC user. I am totally stuck for ideas.

Martin

creator: ramonsdave
content:
Since the last TB ESR update to 128.8 I no longer can download emails properly. It does kick of email download when starting the app, but gets stuck when loading emails from a GMX account. It also does not delete emails from the server as configured to do, so next app start it downloads the same emails again.

Clicking the Get Messages button does nothing nor does clicking on any commands in the context menu of the Get Messages button. There is no status showing in the status bar at the bottom nor do I get an error. I tried it in troubleshooting mode with everything disabled and it still does not work.

I did clear startup cache, compacted mailboxes, and used web mail portals to lower the number of emails to download (I deleted all those emails I don't want anyway).

Used to work reliably well before update and also when scheduled. Troubleshooting Info is below.

title: Is it possible to include an image, or use a different font, in the Subject Line
creator: sweettasha
content:
The Subject Line is the first thing the recipient sees. So it's important that it be consistent with the message's text.

  • I may want to insert an emoji

For example, the Subject Line may be humorous. "You take yourself too seriously". Insert a LOL emoji

  • I may want to use a different font

The style of a font can suggest the theme or basic sentiment of the text.

Thanks for your help, SweetTasha

title: OAuth2 with Google workplace works with Windows but not with Linux Mint
creator: tsepati
content:
My host institution recently made a contract with Google and now we all use Google Workspace; our institutional e-mail server (Zimbra) is replaced with GMail. My full emailadress is the username, and then Google forwards one to a log-in-page of the institution via OAuth2.

At the institute I use Thunderbird on WIndows, and it is able to access the new account, as expected.

But here at home, where I use Linux Mint (Ubuntu/Debian based), also with Thunderbird, it doesn't work. I think the configuration is correct (it is identical to the one I use at the workplace) (first attached screenshot)

But when it's supposed to open the institution's authentication window, it gets stuck in a previous step, which is the Google window that should pass/redirect to the institutional log-in window via oAuth2. (second attached screenshot)

But instead, from here it goes to an unknown error, according to Google. (third attached screenshot)

I don't know what functionality I possibly haven't installed or activated in Thunderbird here at home, but which is functional on Windows? Or whether some security/privacy switch of Thunderbird or Linux' firewall or whatever, is 'on' and is blocking the redirection to the third-party log-in window?

How do I (in)activate or change what needs to be (in)activated or changed? Any ideas? Where can I look for this information? I scanned the questions on OAuth2 here, and none seemed related to what I experience.

It's important for me that I access my work email from home. Please help.

title: Horizontal scrolling when replying
creator: mozilla98
content:
When I reply to an HTML message, a horizontal scroll bar appears. Horizontal scrolling or widening the window is necessary to read the message that I am responding to. But sometimes I must widen the window to an extreme width, which I don't like to do, or scroll horizontally to read the text, which I don't like to do. I end up having to look at the original message in a separate window in order to write a response to it because I cannot read it easily in the quoted text area of the composition window.

Other e-mail programs wrap text better within a window of any width and they do not require horizontal scrolling.

The only way I know to deal with this problem in Thunderbird is to use the re-wrap menu option. But even that does not get rid of horizontal scrolling, and I do not like the fixed line length that it imposes. I want text to wrap dynamically in windows, as it does in, well, just about every text handling program that I've used in the last 40 years.

Is there a better way to eliminate the need for horizontal scrolling? Something in configuration settings? Something in CSS?

In case it matters: I write plain-text messages.

title: Google Calendar Broke Today - New Event Greyed out - Accounts will not import calendars
creator: josh98
content:
This is a strange issue. I've been using Thunderbird for over 15 years, and today, while working with a client, I attempted to click "New Event," but it was greyed out. After ending the call, I started troubleshooting.

Steps I’ve Taken:

  • Re-added all existing accounts—no change.
  • Tried adding a fresh calendar—received a strange error (which I didn’t copy) about encryption or a "request missing required authentication credential."

title: Message preview problem
creator: Nasshorn
content:
I just reinstalled Windows and then Thunderbird. I'm getting the list of messages but no message preview pane as I'm accustomed to. I found out I can hit the F8 and turn it on/off, but it appears to the side. I like it underneath the message list. I see instructions to click on the "View" to set it up, but I cannot find where this alleged "View" menu item is. I've tried to find it in Settings with no success. Please help me get my message preview pane back as it was in the bottom portion of the screen.

Thanks

When leaving the default, the account also will not get any messages. Are there different settings that I need to change that I am unaware of? Thank you.

title: message from Mozilla about loss of account
creator: lora.r.smith
content:
Hi, Mozilla. I got an email from you (or someone pretending to be you) about losing access to browsing products like Firefox sync, Mozilla Monitor, Firefox Relay, and MDN.

I am wondering is this a real message or spam?

I have been a Mozilla customer since your offices were in the Landmark buildings in Mountain View (now part of Google). I do on occasion use Firefox as a browser, but am not asked for a password, so I assume I'm automatically signed in. However, I use Thunderbird nearly daily as my main email account. I also don't sign in to that because it doesn't ask me to, and this must be how the account is set up (sorry I don't remember exactly; that was a while ago).

So if this message is really from you, am I at risk of losing my Thunderbird account? If so, I have a lot of work to do transferring to something else.

Thanks for your answer.

Lora R. Smith
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/134.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

title: Checks account several times, checks accounts it is not asked to check
creator: jandi
content: I have the latest Thunderbird (128.8.1) on my win 10 pc. I have 3 email accounts On start up it is set to check for emails - it checks account 1, 3 times, accounts 2 & 3 twice each. If i select file - get new messages for.... and choose an account, it will check all accounts when account 1 is selected, some of them multiple times, it will check accounts 1 and 2 when account 2 is selected, and it will check account 3 twice whe account 3 is selected. It successfully retirieves al emails from ll accounts. It has been working correctly (just checks all 3 accounts at startup once each) for years, but just recently, it has assumed this behaviour. Not sure if it is related to a recent update. Any ideas ? thank you
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:136.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/136.0;kb_visits_prior:["/en-US/kb/using-multiple-profiles"]

title: advocacy emails
creator: ztem
content: I have noticed for about two weeks that emails from known non-profit sites requesting advocacy responses/comments are becoming increasingly slow to connect or not connecting at all and that they are being flagged as insecure in Thunderbird. Is this new? Is it, in fact caused by Mozilla, as a matter of security? Are there bad actors mucking about? The slow/no response thing is frustrating since I don't have a lot of time to engage in this activity. My internet connection is via GreenLight. Thanks for any enlightenment. Emily Metz

title: external input to mail program
creator: hrsweet3
content:
I am only able to send an email by opening Thunderbird and selecting write.

If I try to attach a file by starting with the file > send to > mail recipient or try to trigger Thunderbird with an email address in a browser or start with a distribution list in the address book, all fail.

All produce the following diagnostic: "Sending of the message failed. The message could not be sent because connecting to Outgoing server (SMTP) localhost failed. The server may be unavailable or is refusing SMTP connections. Please verify that your outgoing server (SMTP) settings are correct and try again."

title: Calendar Invite Thund.>> Outlook
creator: info12779
content:
Hi everyone, I use Thunderbird with the integrated calendar extension to invite people to meetings. Within Thunderbird, the message and invitation arrive just fine: you can read everything when the email arrives in the normal message area. However, when I send the invitation to clients using Outlook, they only see an empty email with the option to accept the invitation (or not). Once they accept, the email disappears from their inbox (which is probably a different problem). More importantly, when they open the appointment in their calendar, they have to click on an attachment before they see the description (i.e. the link to an online meeting).

It seems that the invitation sent by Thunderbird is not fully integrated.

Does anyone know how to fix this? Looking forward to your response,

title: profile location on Ubuntu
creator: dannygarside
content:
[The documentation](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/profiles-where-thunderbird-stores-user-data#w_finding-your-profile-without-opening-thunderbird) says that my profile should be at `home/[user]/.thunderbird`. It is not there.

In settings the "Open Directory" button does nothing (cursor spins and then nothing happens).

In `about:profiles` I see the profile listed at `/home/[user]/.thunderbird/[XXX]`. That folder does not exist (so far as I can tell (I have hidden files turned on). The _Local Directory_ version _does_ exist (`/home/[user]/.var/app/org.mozilla.Thunderbird/cache/thunderbird/[XXX]`)

What's going on? Am I doing something wrong?

title: Understanding syncing of the address book with Google Contacts
creator: rlytleltc
content:
I installed Thunderbird 136.0.1 for Windows this week to use with my Gmail/Google account. I'm loving the way it works and the fact that it interfaces with the Google calendars (even the birthday calendar driven by Google contacts) well. The address book seems a bit more inconsistent with syncing/updating and I'm trying to gain an understanding of how that works. If I add a contact the the address book in Thunderbird, it shows up in the web interface in Google contacts. However, If I delete a contact in the web interface, that contact is not removed from the Thunderbird address book. Am I just not being patient enough for the syncing to take place?

Thanks! Rob

title: Can't launch Thunderbird on one Mac user account - different behavior
creator: cbelov
content:
I previously posted [launch Thunderbird on one Mac user account] and the solution by @DavidGG worked then. But it isn't working this time. So I really need to know what file triggers the Thunderbird Install Helper dialog on macOS.

Here are the steps for the current situation:

Steps: 1. On user 2, launch Thunderbird. 2. Get the dialog that there's an update to 128.8.0esr. 3. Click the Restart to update button. 4. Get the dialog that there's an update to 128.8.0esr. 5. Click the Restart to update button. 6. Get the dialog to enter admin ID and password for installing the Thunderbird helper. 7. Enter the admin ID and password and click OK.

Expected result: Thunderbird relaunches, now with 128.8.1esr. Actual result: Get the dialog to enter admin ID and password for installing the Thunderbird helper.

10. Log off user 2 and log on to user 1.

Here is where I did something different from the previous issue. Last time I deleted Thunderbird and re-installed it. This time I did the upgrade.

11. On user 1, launch Thunderbird. 12. Get the dialog that there's an update to 128.8.0esr. 13. Click the Restart to update button. 14. Get the dialog that there's an update to 128.8.0esr. 15. Click the Restart to update button. 16. Get the dialog to enter admin ID and password for installing the Thunderbird helper. 17. Enter the admin ID and password and click OK.

Expected and actual result: Thunderbird launches and I can use it normally.

18. Log out of user 1 and log into user 2. 19. Launch Thunderbird

Expected result: Thunderbird launches and I can use it normally. Actual result: Get the dialog to enter admin ID and password for installing the Thunderbird helper.

I'm guessing there's some manner of residual file that's preventing Thunderbird from launching on user 2. I've verified Thunderbird is in the system's Application folder and not in user 1's files.

So I continued with the recommended remediation from the previous issue:

20. Trash ~/Library/Thunderbird 21. Launch Thunderbird

Expected result: Thunderbird launches and I can proceed recover profiles.ini and the Profiles folder from the trash Actual result: Get the dialog to enter admin ID and password for installing the Thunderbird helper.

How do I clear the pending install from user 2 so that Thunderbird will launch for user 2? What is the specific file or code line that triggers the request to install the helper?

(Yes, I did move my files back out of the trash so I don't lose them. I also have a TimeMachine backup.)

I suppose I could totally re-install Thunderbird like I did the other time, but I'm hoping for a less time-intensive solution. I guess I could also dust off my old programming skills and dive into the source code - an advantage of Open Source - but it's not a strength for me and Thunderbird is coded in a language I'm not fluent in.

title: Thunderbird 115.x - Can't Delete Messages
creator: dunogod
content:
Running Thunderbird 115.10.x on Windows 10 Pro 22H2 on an older Samsung Note 9 Pro 15 w/ an 8th gen Intel i7, 16GB RAM and an aftermarket 1TB SSD w/ over 660GB of free space. I have emails going back to 1997 in the Sent folder (16K sent emails). While running Thunderbird 115.9.x earlier today, I noticed I suddenly could not delete emails out of my Inbox. The following error message was displayed:

The messages could not be moved or copied to folder 'Trash on [username@domain.com]' because writing to folder failed. To gain disk space, from the File menu, first choose Empty Trash, and then choose Compact Folders, and then try again.

So I did that to no avail. I then tried to delete messages out of other folders as a test (Sent, Drafts, etc.) figuring I could simply move them back if successful. Received the same error message. Closed all applications and rebooted the laptop as it had been running for several days. Same error message.

Did some research which all told me to delete the Trash MBOX files (including the .msf file). Did that, same result. Launched in Safe Mode, same result. Created a Trash.txt file, removed the .txt file extension and relaunched Thunderbird. Same result. Compacted all folders and relaunched. Same result.

If I backup the current folders and restore from them, I'll get the same result. Anyone have a new idea or set of instructions I can follow?

Thanks!

title: Giving Permission to Google to Access Thunderbird Account
creator: rabbithutch1
content:
From time to time - and for reasons that I cannot discern - I get screens popping up from Google saying that it needs information or permission to access T-bird accounts. I have both gmail and other domain mail server accounts in T-bird.

Today, it's happening again. I can't seem to resolve the issue even though I've been through the process of providing requested information and permissions. title: Can Thunderbird replace my Outlook scenario?
creator: BoulderRidge
content:
I'm looking for an email solution for my husband and Thunderbird looks promising. However I'd really appreciate input from this community regarding our specific scenario before I make a move. I have a programming background but I fumble blindly trying to be his email admin.

The problem we are trying to solve is his current email setup chokes on send/receive almost daily, after which he has to use Task Manager to kill a hanging Outlook.exe process. After years of tinkering I'm about to give up on Outlook completely. I think the main issue is how Outlook interacts with his Archive. His Archive.pst file is 4GB and Outlook saves it as one big file even though it has many subfolders which are essentially his filing system. Outlook is configured to save everything directly to a local laptop which is backed up regularly and we want to retain this local feature. Thunderbird is attractive because: 1. it supports the local drive storage 2. it appears to have options for transferring his Outlook archive and other folders over in such a way that he can continue to search and access them as needed. He would also like to be able to: 1. move new emails received in Thunderbird into the same (transferred) archive subfolders he has now 2. send and receive new emails without the program choking 3. view and send new emails from his Android phone (without accessing the archive) 4. transfer his email contacts from Outlook and manage them in Thunderbird Can anyone see red flags with this in Thunderbird? He does NOT use: 

- Outlook Calendar (we synch our phone calendars to Google calendar online) - Outlook Tasks

Technical Details (in case it matters): 

- HP Pavilion 2021 laptop running Windows 10 with 1TB storage (200+ GB free) and 16GB RAM - Likely to upgrade to a newer bigger laptop with Windows 11 this year - Outlook is running from a Microsoft 365 subscription - His actual email account is from gmail but is named per a domain name I purchased - His Outlook account is setup using IMAP - We use ESET for virus protection and NordVPN for WIFI security

Any comments or advice are greatly appreciated!

title: Large message corrupted in Thunderbird after sending
creator: marck1
content:
I sent a message with 2 large attachments, which came to about 700kb. I have my email account configured to move replies to the original message folder after sending. The sent message appears blank in the folder. View source reveals little but starts with the following: X-Mozilla-Status: 0001 X-Mozilla-Status2: 00000000 IesvbLYCv+2WRXVrCMCm89IOemygPZpNbz2NBY [and so on for 433 lines of 73 bytes], < 32kb.

When I use my online webmail interface, the message that Thunderbird can no longer read is intact, legible and contains both the large attachments. The same is true when viewed in another email client. I have restored the message by exporting it from the webmail and importing it back to Thunderbird (to keep my records intact), which Thunderbird handles okay.

The outgoing mail server log shows that the message received from Thunderbird and sent to the final recipient was the correct 700kb+ size.

I'm rather curious as to what went on there.

Attachments to post: 1. View of the corrupt message in the Trash folder 2. Intact message after importing as an export from webmail.

Thanks!!

title: How to view only one message in preview pane
creator: ugcheleuce
content:
Hello everyone

I recently upgraded from TB 69 to the latest TB (after a crucial add-on finally got updated), and I'm generally happy with it, except for one little irk:

In Classic layout, the preview pane now contain multiple messages. As far as I can tell, it contains all messages of the selected thread, in date order (not in message list order). I would prefer the preview pane to contain just ONE message, namely the currently selected message. It is annoying to have to click repeatedly inside the preview pane to access the individual messages in the preview pane. It is also visually confusing (to me).

What's worse, the messages in the preview pane do not follow the normal threading: they appear to be shown in order of date, not in the order of the messages in the message list.

In the screenshot, you can see the thread has 11 messages, and in the message list there are three subthreads. The preview pane also shows the 11 messages, but not in the same order as in the message list. Plus, in order for me to read any messages, I need to click inside the preview pane to fold each message in or out.

I prefer a situation where I can click a single message in the message list, and a single message is shown in the preview pane. Is this possible with the latest TB?

Thanks.

title: No Calendar reminders
creator: ajhajh2003
content:
I recently installed Thunderbird on a new Windows 11 PC. Everything works except calendar reminders. My Thunderbird profile lives on the D drive so I copied my old profile.ini file which points to my Thunderbird profile on the D drive from the old pc at C:\Users\"ownwer"\AppData\Roaming\Thunderbird to the corresponding folder on the new PC. I have the latest version of Thunderbird, 136.0.1. The old PC was using Thunderbird version 115.5.1. When I tried to revert to version 115.12.0 I got an error message about the profile incompatibility between versions that related to version 68 i.e. "Unable to use profile when you launch an older version of Thunderbird". Please help.

Fixed. The problem seems to be caused because the new version defaulted to not showing reminders for writable calendars. Click on the burger symbol at the top right of the calendar window and choose Settings. Scroll past what is immediately visible to the bottom of the window to find the setting for "Show missed reminders for writable calendars" and select. This setting is not available for individual calendars when you view their properties by right clicking on the calendar name in the bottom left window but the "Show Reminders" must also be selected here to see reminders.

title: Reverting to threaded messages
creator: jsg11
content:
I'm using the latest update of Thunderbird on a Windows 11 machine. This issue has been present since I started using TB sometime last year, so through various updates.

I set most of my folders, especially those used regularly (inbox, sent, trash, etc.) to unthreaded, which does sort the messages unthreaded. (Some folders I use so infrequently I haven't bothered to change them)

However, in many cases (mostly excluding the inbox), the folder reverts to threaded and I have to do it all over again.

Not the end of the world, but aggravating.

In an ideal world, I'd set ALL of my folders to unthreaded and probably leave them all there forever. (Although the option to switch on the fly does seem nice and conceivably, I might use it). Reading previous threads, I'm assuming that this needs to be done folder by folder. But what I have NOT seen is why folders which have been changed revert to threaded. I haven't figured out any pattern to it; reversions don't happen every time, so they appear to be rather random.

P.S. I have 2 email accounts and at various times, I've had to delete and re-install both of them. For one, it happens with some frequency, for the other, much less so. But I'm pretty certain that there is no correlation between the problem described and reloading the accounts. And I'm also pretty certain that it occurs from time to time in folders which store emails which are independent of either account and not online.

title: Calendar .vcs invitations being treated as text, not functioning, when Google Calendar enabled
creator: fred37
content:
One of the best things about Thunderbird is that it has been compatible with the Outlook-style .vcs calendar invite attachments ever since Lightning. Now it's integral. Until this week it was great -- I get a lot of meeting invites via calendar attachments, and a fair number of cancellations too. When I clicked on the mail I got a dialog box asking if I wanted to add or delete the meeting, or saying that it already has been.

But suddenly it's not working. (This is using 128.6.esr and then 128.8.1.) Instead, the decoded text of the invite is now being displayed as a text message, and it doesn't give me any way to add or delete the invite unless I do it manually. The attachment is not being processed.

The problem seems somehow related to Provider for Google Calendar. I use that so my phone can make calendar entries. It has worked for years with no problem, but the problem I'm describing goes away if I disable Provider for Google Calendar, in which case I don't see a lot of events entered that way, or set it to read only, in which case I can't enter things my cell phone can see, which I used to be able to do. Yeah, I sure wish the two calendar systems could be better integrated so my cell phone could see the Home calendar or the attachments could be directed to Google Calendar, but no go.

A few days ago, my Home calendar disappeared from T'bird, leaving only the Google one visible. I re-enabled it and everything came back, but the attachments don't work when everything is normally enabled. Maybe that's related. Suggestions?

title: doppia posta inviata con email differenti
creator: francescopesci5
content: su due pc diversi aventi lo stesso account di google su TB si sono create 2 cartelle di posta inviata contenenti email differenti. Usando lo stesso account alcune mail vanno in una cartella di posta inviata altre mail nell'altra cartella. Ho provato a cancellare il database e ho riconfigurato la mail ex-novo il risultato che ha ricreato si nuovo 2 cartelle di posta inviata sebbene sul server di gmail ci sia una sola cartella di posta inviata.

title: Error when sending S/MIME encrypted email
creator: mmeggie.nguyen
content:
Hi guys,

I'm communicating with some colleagues using S/MIME encrypted messages. I have already imported all the necessary licenses, and all licenses are valid until 2034. I can receive and read all the encrypted messages they sent me, but every time I try to reply or send them (the same people) encrypted messages, the error pops up saying 'End-to-end encryption requires resolving certificate issues'. I already checked all the licenses but couldn't find any issues. Can you suggest some ways to resolve it? Thank you!

content: I've been using Thunderbird for years (decades), and just installed a new version. content:
I am finally making the move to ditch Microsoft Outlook for good and use Thunderbird as my daily driver for all my email needs.
